findings.notes: CONFIRMED: Transaction-wrapped vec0 writes (delete+insert per row into vec_memories) at lines 370-381. Called from writeVectorsToShard which also creates vec0 virtual table. Blocks Turso migration.

CONFIRMED: Probes sqlite_master for memory_fts table existence. Used by shouldWarmInMemoryBm25 and shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine to gate FTS5 vs in-memory BM25. Turso migration must replace this check.

findings.signature: canonical.exec(`ATTACH DATABASE ... AS legacy`); canonical.exec(`ATTACH DATABASE ... AS shard`);
findings.notes: CONFIRMED: ATTACH/DETACH used for 3-database migration (legacy→canonical→shard) at lines 243-244, 272-273. Turso has no ATTACH — requires architectural redesign.
CONFIRMED: Creates vec0 virtual table in attached shard (line 72-75) and copies rows. Uses ATTACH, vec0, and loadExtension. Turso has none of these.
CONFIRMED: FTS5 'delete-all' command used in clearTable(). Turso/libSQL lacks FTS5; must be reimplemented.
CONFIRMED: FTS5 'rebuild' command used in post-restore derived artifact rebuilds (fts-rebuild step). FTS5 not available in Turso.
CONFIRMED: v2 restore is entirely file-copy based (fs.renameSync live→backup, fs.copyFileSync snapshot→live) at lines 2689-2707. Turso remote DB cannot fs.copyFileSync.
CONFIRMED: VACUUM INTO on attached database (active_vec). Turso lacks ATTACH and VACUUM INTO; vector shard snapshot needs complete reimplementation.
CONFIRMED: VACUUM INTO is SQLite-specific; used as core v2 checkpoint snapshot mechanism. Turso/libSQL does not support it.

findings.signature: verifyFts5Isolation(db): boolean — probes sqlite_master for memory_fts
findings.notes: CONFIRMED: FTS5 virtual table existence probe in verifyFts5Isolation. Checks if memory_fts exists and verifies learned_triggers not in FTS5. Turso lacks FTS5.
CONFIRMED: Direct better-sqlite3 driver import. Every call site (initDb, getDb, etc.) couples to synchronous better-sqlite3 API. Turso uses @libsql/client.
CONFIRMED: Three .pragma() calls at lines 299-301. libSQL lacks .pragma() method — must use db.exec('PRAGMA ...') or connection string params.
CONFIRMED: loadExtension in reindex shard writer (writeVectorsToShard). Turso blocks extension loading — reindex cannot populate vec0 KNN table.
CONFIRMED: Three .pragma() calls at lines 147-149. libSQL has no .pragma() API — requires exec-based pragma or connection config.
CONFIRMED: FTS5 optimize command after bulk deletes in runPostDeleteMaintenance. Turso has no FTS5 — must remove or replace.
CONFIRMED: loadExtension during shard migration at line 242. Turso blocks extension loading — migration logic cannot create vec0 tables.
CONFIRMED: sqlite-vec scalar function used in vector_search (line 314), multi_concept_search (lines 403, 407). Turso has no vec_distance_cosine — must compute externally.

findings.signature: FTS5 bm25() ranking function with per-column weights
findings.notes: REFUTED: No bm25() function call found in vector-index-schema.ts. The file contains schema DDL/migration logic, not BM25 query functions. The bm25() function is referenced in bm25-index.ts in-memory implementation. FTS5 bm25() ranking is not directly called in this file.

findings.symbol: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5
findings.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text, content='memory_index', content_rowid='id')
findings.notes: CONFIRMED: FTS5 virtual table creation with 3 sync triggers in migration v14 (lines 1256-1281). Turso lacks FTS5 — full-text search must use external engine.

findings.signature: memory_fts FTS5 virtual table referenced in schema migrations
findings.notes: CONFIRMED: FTS5 virtual table for BM25 full-text search. Created in migration v14 and rebuilt in migration v33. Turso lacks FTS5.

findings.signature: vec_distance_cosine used via sqlite-vec extension loaded by sqliteVec.load(database)
findings.notes: CONFIRMED: sqlite-vec loaded at line 21 and used throughout for KNN queries. vec_distance_cosine is the core distance function. Must be reimplemented for Turso.
CONFIRMED: FTS5 delete-all and rebuild commands used in checkpoint operations. Turso/libSQL has no FTS5.
CONFIRMED: VACUUM INTO on attached shard. Turso cannot attach databases or VACUUM INTO; snapshot strategy must change entirely.
CONFIRMED: VACUUM INTO is the core v2 checkpoint snapshot mechanism. Turso/libSQL lacks this — must replace with serialize()/file-copy or Turso snapshot API.
CONFIRMED: Full FTS5 virtual table creation with content-sync triggers and rebuild (lines 203-235). Turso has no FTS5 or loadExtension — blocks schema downgrade path.
CONFIRMED: Turso/libSQL has no .pragma() method; busy_timeout must be set via connection config or PRAGMA SQL statement if supported.
CONFIRMED: WAL triplet file model. All copy/move/restore logic (copyTriplet, moveTriplet, restoreTriplet) assumes local files. Turso uses remote replicas — all triplet logic becomes obsolete.
CONFIRMED: integrity_check pragma in runIntegrityCheck is local SQLite. Turso has no embedded integrity check — recovery procedure CG-RP-001 must be redesigned.

findings.signature: async function retryEmbedding(id, content, claimedPreviousStatus): Promise<RetryResult>
findings.notes: CONFIRMED: retryEmbedding writes to vec_memories (vec0 virtual table) at lines 756-762. Turso/libSQL has NO vector index support — this is a migration blocker.

findings.signature: WITH RECURSIVE causal_walk(origin_id, node_id, hop_distance, walk_score) AS (...)
findings.notes: CONFIRMED: Recursive CTE with bidirectional UNION seed + UNION ALL recursive step traversing causal_edges up to MAX_HOPS(2). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE.
CONFIRMED: Core recursive CTE for graph traversal with bidirectional edges and relation-weighted scoring. Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must rewrite as iterative application-side traversal.

findings.signature: WITH RECURSIVE causal_walk(origin_id, node_id, hop_distance, walk_score) AS (SELECT ... UNION SELECT ... UNION ALL SELECT ...)
findings.notes: CONFIRMED: Recursive CTE for 2-hop graph traversal. Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must be replaced with iterative application-side traversal or bounded expansion.

findings.signature: WITH RECURSIVE dependents(child_path) AS (SELECT ... UNION SELECT ...)

findings.signature: WITH RECURSIVE dependents(child_path) AS (SELECT child_path FROM dependency_edges WHERE parent_path IN (...) UNION SELECT edge.child_path FROM dependency_edges edge JOIN dependents dep ON edge.parent_path = dep.child_path)
